question.text: What is the market-wide circuit breaker limit that triggers trading halt for the rest of the day?

answer.text: 20 percent decline in the index at any time during market hours
10 percent decline in the index before 1 PM
10 percent decline in the index after 2:30 PM
5 percent decline in the index at any time

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A 20% decline in either BSE Sensex or Nifty 50 (whichever is breached first) at any time during market hours triggers a halt for the rest of the day. No pre-open session follows a 20% trigger. **The Trap:** A 10% decline before 1 PM (option b) causes only a 45-minute halt, not rest-of-day. A 10% decline after 2:30 PM (option c) does NOT trigger any halt at all. A 5% decline (option d) is not a circuit breaker level — the levels are 10%, 15%, and 20%. **Cross-Exam Context:** 10% trigger: Before 1 PM = 45 min halt; 1-2:30 PM = 15 min halt; After 2:30 PM = no halt. 15% trigger: Before 1 PM = 1h 45min; 1-2 PM = 45 min; After 2 PM = rest of day. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Circuit breaker levels: 10%, 15%, 20%. The 20% is the nuclear option - always means rest of day closure regardless of time. Based on previous day's closing level.
